entry.title: Re: Which is the catalog assigned to App "Assign Payment Type to a Company Code"?
entry.content: Does anyone know which catalog is needed to use "Assign Payment Type to a Company Code" (107024)? Thanks.

entry.title: Supplier evaluation by inspection lot
entry.content:
Hello, 

How does the supplier evaluation works via inspection lots, the criterion is grayed out and I cannot modify it 

how its calculated?

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Substitution rule reference bkpf-xref by PO number
entry.content:
Dear all,

We are going to do self invoicing for PO with periodic invoicing plan using tile create invoicing plan settlement (MRIS). It is working fine, however to allow our supplier to know what was paid we would like to fill the PO number in the field BKPF-XREF for the accounting document of the MM invoices.

I created a substitution rule on header level with prerequisite tcode = MRIS with a user exit. The rule is correctly triggered how the reference is not fed. Here is the user exit

"

form u301.
  data(lv_mm_invoice) = conv belnr_d( bkpf-xblnr ).

  if lv_mm_invoice is initial.
    return.
  endif.

  select distinct ekpo~ebeln,
                  fplt~afdat,
                  fplt~fkdat
    from fplt
           inner join
             ekpo on  ekpo~fplnr = fplt~fplnr
                  and ekpo~loekz = @Space
               inner join
                 rseg on  rseg~ebeln = ekpo~ebeln
                      and rseg~belnr = @LV_mm_invoice
    where fplt~afdat <= @SY-DATUM
      and fplt~fksaf  = @c_inv_plan_item_status-open
    into table @DATA(lt_po_invoicing_dates).

  if sy-subrc <> 0.
    return.
  endif.

"

It is not working because  bkpf-xref is not filled with the MM document directly but with value "*MM". So is there please another way to get the PO number ? Or to get the MM document number to get the PO ?

Do you please have any other suggestion on how to fill the PO number in bkpf-xref ? Knowing that according sap documentation rules on complete document don't work with fiori

Best regards

entry.title: 🇺🇸 ABAP AI Capabilities in SAP Joule for Developers (York, United States)
entry.content:
In this CodeJam you will learn how to use ABAP capabilities in SAP Joule for Developers, including predictive code completion, unit test generation for ABAP CDS and ABAP code, and code explanation.

Speed up your development with ABAP Cloud using SAP Joule's AI-assisted features: predictive code completion that understands ABAP Cloud semantics, automatic unit test generation for CDS views and ABAP classes, and natural language code explanation to understand unfamiliar code quickly.

Location and Date:

This is a free in-person event (not virtual) and is planned for Thursday, August 13th, 2026 from 10:00 - 16:00 EST in York, Pennsylvania, United States. The language of the event and the content will be English.

Event Address: The Yorktowne Hotel, 48 East Market Street, York, Pennsylvania, 17401, United States

The instructor is @RichHeilman.

The co-instructor is @thomas_jung.

The event is kindly hosted by SAP. Thank you!

Prerequisites:

Install ABAP Development Tools (ADT) in Eclipse on your local machine before the event: https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/abap-install-adt.html

Please note that spaces are limited! We can only accommodate 30 attendees. If you want to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right. A confirmation email will be sent to all registered participants 1 week before the event.

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: RDS SAP GUI and SAP business client
entry.content:
We are deploying an On-Premises SAP ECC / S4HANA system and need clarification on official support for client delivery.
- Based on SAP Note 66971 and help.sap.com guides for Terminal Server, does SAP officially support Microsoft RemoteApp, App Delivery Citrix, and App Delivery VMware Horizon, Remote Desktop Protocol , and VDI .  for delivering SAP GUI and SAP Business Client to end-users?

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 16 - Escaped names
Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 05 - Portable functions
Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 17 - Schema evolution testing
SAP Enable Now - Screen misalignments in Documentation output
entry.content:
Hi All,
We did transactions in GUI and the Author struggled with the screen size when using an additional monitor. Subsequently, the screen image size was adjusted manually when the simulation was edited in Producer. Now, the problem is that the instruction bubbles and pointers/numbers in the document display, is misaligned. The simulation with all the different modes plays correctly, it's only the documentation output that is incorrect.

1. How can I fix this?
2. Where do I find document style without overlay bubbles for reliability? Would this help?
3. Where can i fix to "text below screenshot" - would this help?
I don't see options for "relative positioning" or 'recalculate bubble positions".
I only need a work-around for one course without re-recording but to get the instructions aligned at the right place. Screenshot below:

entry.title: SAP Cloud Public Edition, Bank Communication Management
entry.content:
Dear all,

I am testing Advanced Payment Management on SAP S/4HANA CLOUD 2602 and the objective is to activate BCM flow. Please find below an example of the process (please refer to the documentation Grouping | SAP Help Portal):

  • we are uploading two payment medium files into the system
  • each upload generates an incoming payment order and an outgoing payment order which are displayed in "Manage Payments" app
  • according to settings defined under the Payment Agreement, the two outgoing payment orders are collected into the same "Object List Number"
  • the object list is processed automatically via a job 

After this last step, a payment batch is created and displayed within the "Approve bank payments" app but the assigned Rule is not the one defined in customizing:

In this customizing step, clicking on the Rule ID matchcode, different rules are displayed, but I cannot find the step in which they are maintained: 

I would appreciate your help.

Thank you.

Best Regards.

entry.title: Re: Implementing Get_expanded_entity/ Get_expanded_entityset
entry.content:
DATA(ls_key_tab) =  it_key_tab[ name = 'EmpId' ].
IF sy-subrc = 0.
      DATA(lemp_id) = ls_key_tab-value.
ENDIF.

Hi,

I had a question regarding table expressions in modern ABAP.

As per my understanding, when we use a table expression like:

DATA(ls_key_tab) =  it_key_tab[ name = 'EmpId' ].

and the specified entry is not found, the runtime raises the exception  CX_SY_ITAB_LINE_NOT_FOUND . Because of this, I was under the impression that sy-subrc  is not set in such cases.

However, I came across code where  sy-subrc is checked immediately after a table expression. Could you please help me understand the rationale behind this approach? Is there any specific scenario where checking sy-subrc after a table expression is meaningful, or am I missing something in my understanding?

I would appreciate your guidance.

entry.title: Re: Which is the catalog assigned to App "Assign Payment...
entry.content:
Try business catalog SAP_CA_BC_IC_LND_FIN_AP0_PC

Sources

  • KBA 3192089 - "No authorization to start the UI" error raised for SSCUI 101044 
  • KBA 3609821 - Customer Unable to Update Payment per Due Date

entry.title: Re: EU Pay Transparency in SAP SuccessFactors — A Suite-Level Compliance Playbook

We have the same issue where we use the multilocation job posting feature but only need to advertise the salary range for one of the countries and not the others.

entry.title: Re: Submitting XML via Mail with SAP DRC and SAP ERP
entry.content:
Hello Maurice,

Yes, indeed—within the SAP EDOC_COCKPIT, you can configure two communication channels: one via Peppol and the other via Email. Both variants leverage SAP DRC for the transmission of electronic documents.

Using the customizing tables SM30 → EDOEUBUPADOCTYPV and EDOEUBUPAV, you can define which communication channel and content type is assigned to each customer.

Since you already have the correct reference for setting up the SAP DRC email scenario, the next step is to activate and configure the required connections and parameters as described in the official documentation.

However, before proceeding with the implementation, it is strongly recommended to first apply all relevant SAP Notes for the application components:

  • CA-GTF-CSC-EDO-DE
  • CA-GTF-CSC-EDO-PAP

I hope this helps you move forward with your implementation.

Best Regards, Günter

entry.title: Re: TM Public Cloud - transportation scheduling without 46P
entry.content:
Good day @joannab 

Thank you for your question.

Your understanding is partly correct: you do not require 46P to create or manually schedule a freight order. However, it is required to activate the standard integration with SAP HANA spatial services for automatic geocoding and automatic distance and duration determination.

See Managing Locations and Geographical Information for more insights.

The planned dates can still appear because departure and arrival dates do not necessarily prove that SAP has calculated a road distance.

Scheduling determines the start and end times for freight order activities, including departure, arrival, loading, and unloading. These dates can be influenced by the time windows received from the freight unit or preceding busines document.

In the Starter System, the dates may therefore originate from the following:

  • Pickup and delivery time windows transferred to the freight unit
  • Dates already available in the sales order or delivery
  • Manually maintained freight order dates
  • Distance and duration values previously stored in the distance and duration buffer

See the following reference for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/61e246f4b34c4e1790d8b7651c0b40a8/199883529a4a7b6ae10000000a423f68.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/61e246f4b34c4e1790d8b7651c0b40a8/513060d226c44f299c634fd4b727e546.html?locale=en-US

An empty distance field in the location does not confirm that no distance or duration exists. Distance and duration are determined for a pair of locations and can be stored in the buffer rather than directly in the individual location master.

Without the SAP HANA spatial services integration, the standard options you have are:

  1. Manually maintain or adjust the planned dates and time in the freight order.
  2. Use Pickup and delivery time windows inherited from the originating sales order, delivery, or freight unit.
  3. Receive planned dates, distances, or durations from an external transportation planning system through the released freight order integration.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/61e246f4b34c4e1790d8b7651c0b40a8/31058013fa80465bb6587cf9dba4f861.html?locale=en-US

I recommend you open Manage Freight Orders and compare the freight order stage duration with the freight unit pickup and delivery time windows.

Also check whether the stage contains a distance value. See Manage Freight Orders on how the app automatically calculates distance and duration.

 

Let me know if you have further question.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Approach for SAP Cloud ALM feature tester authorizati...
entry.content: Hi Thea, I think imp_cdm_Tester really just includes the authorization for the action (button), so you need an addtional role which grants edit authorization for the Features. The Project Viewer does not include that. So you need at least Project Memember.

entry.title: Re: ABAP test cockpit (ATC) recommendations for governance of clean core ABAP development
entry.content:
Hello, 

Anyone know why ATC on BTP raises the error “Cannot read released APIs in checked system: System ZCA100CIUZYD5IOYP6BO4PTL6I3MVXMA is required to be on release 7.54 or newer,” even though I am providing the value for the releasedAPIDataSource check parameter using the latest version of the released SAP APIs for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition?

 

Thanks and regards,

Gerardo.

entry.title: Re: Asset Accoutning is going live in 2026, however there...
entry.content:
Siva, you are following the right procedure. Why would you move the Asset migration GL balance to retained earnings? together with the open AR/AP/GL upload it will balance out to zero. 

What the client is asking is doable but is not advisable. To do this you will have to make the asset closed FY to be 2024. You will have to make sure that all other aspects of the balance loads concerning the 2026 go live is detailed out and dealt with properly.

I would ask the client the objective of doing what they want and convince them otherwise.

Best regards,

Vishal S Thakur

entry.title: Screen Personas on mobgui for SAP Public Cloud not working on Ivanti Velocity Web Browser
entry.content:
Hi all,

My current customer is using the Ivanti Velocity Web Browser on his Zebra device to run the RF Mobgui in SAP Public Cloud. As some adjustments needed to be done, screen personas was used to make the changes. 

These changes seem to be working fine in any browser when opening the URL, but when the URL is entered in the Velocity Browser the system throws an error "Sorry, the app could not be opened" . Is Ivanti Velocity Web Browser supported for screen personas using Public Cloud? 

 

Thanks for all your help!

entry.title: Re: Add Custom Fields in Look Up Retail Products app
entry.content: did you enable it for the UI in custom field app?

entry.title: Re: New POD 2.0 Framework: Certificate Check
entry.content:
Hi Manoel, the problem with this approach and similar ones, is that all validations are happening with DM logged in user ID. In most cases there are several internal workers ID, where Skills are maintained and used for Labor-On etc. In a realistic scenario you have One to Many between DM Loggin user and Workers.
Ideally the validation should happen somehow when a specific ID try to start the step and not the login account.

entry.title: Re: Your S/4HANA & Ariba implementation is live, but your GRC ruleset do not have Ariba rules co
entry.content: Excellent Blog!

entry.title: Contact for transition of I9s
entry.content:
Our company previously had SAP from 2014 through the end of 2019. During implementation to a new HRIS system, we were able to successfully transfer the I9s, E-Verify and Supporting Documents. However, we were unable to obtain the Audit Trail reflecting that the I9 was electronically signed.

 

Can someone please assist me or refer a contact for this?

 

 

Thanks,

entry.title: Re: Learning token server deprecation (2027)
entry.content:
We had started to transition our users away from the legacy LMS Token Server to the Platform Token Server. However, we are now hearing that OIDC is the preferred solution.

1. We've been working on this migration for a year and a half, this is the first time we're hearing about using OIDC instead of the Platform Token Server. Unfortunately, some of the tools that connect to our LMS via API have already updated, and now we're going to have to ask them to update again. Why was OIDC not communicated earlier? Even the 1H 2026 version of the API documentation states "You should discontinue using the SuccessFactors Learning token server and start using the more secure platform token server" (This exact text is repeated multiple times on pg 612-621) 

2. With that in mind, is there a retirement date for the Platform Token Server? (Or, phrased differently, when will OIDC be mandatory?)

entry.title: Re: Substitution rule reference bkpf-xref by PO number
entry.content:
Did you 

  • check values of BKPF-AWTYP and AWKEY or BSEG--EBELN during execution of the substitution rule
  • consider using a logistic invoice BAdI  to pass information from MM to FI

entry.title: Re: SAP TechEd 2026: Registration is live!
entry.content: Of all the great events SAP puts on, this one has my full attention! Hopefully one day I get to see it in person - even though I'm not a technical guy - just an enthusiast.

entry.title: I vs. AI?
entry.content:
Was talking about it (article: Can AI Agent replace IT Architects (EA/SA)?) – but the topic is just too big (these days?)… There are too many disruptions, not only because AI, but around AI – AI use-caes, controversial water consumption, even locking (or limiting) usage of AI with "decrees". What is next?  Shell, we expect conflicts over AI (or resources needed to power AI Data Centers)?

I guess everything is possible, but aren’t we forgetting something? We are the ones who write the future for the “next” generations… Or we should be the ones who write it!

The plot…

We are already seeing situations, AI is replacing “on masses” many people, in many professions. If we stick to IT, it is well known fact, junior developers have difficult time finding jobs these days – a lot of coding is being done by GenAI (not to go to specific flavors). So, if coders/programs can be “replaced” who else can be replaced?

What about SA or EA?

Of course, an IT Architecture can be observed as a process of reasoning, decision making, and acting accordingly. There is of course a lot of knowledge (experience) and ceremony (frameworks). One may say – nothing we cannot replace with good AI model, RAG and vector database…

While I can argue, things are not so simple (although there is perception, it might be sust so easy), inevitable AI Agents (or Agentic AI) will be put into use to replace IT Architecture work in many aspects. Initially it will come as a “helper” tools (I guess this is how it was “sold” to coders/developers some time ago), then tools will “grow” and become more powerful. At some point of time, our mangers will be confident, AI Agents can take over “bulk” of our work, and start give us more “free” time to work on “other” tasks… Everything else, afterwards – I assume we can guess…

What about “those” managers?

In fact, if we start delegating to AI Agents sensitive Architectural decisions, no doubt senior managers might think of “releasing” some “repetitive” work form the mid-management. I assume building such RAG and vector database might not be so difficult after all…

What about “any” managers?

To go few steps further, why would anyone believe, one day somebody “else” would not build an AI Agent capable of replacing his/her work?

Future is bright?

To go a bit into future (not so distant?), no reason to expect that modern robots and “smart” AI Agents cannot replace majority of current human professions – obviously it’s not only manual factory workers, or various “siting” roles in administration and IT– it could also be lawyers, doctors, teachers… No this is not a trailer for some “B-production” SF movie; this is the reality we are “slowly” rushing into.

Conceptually, what would be the “hold”? Technology will most certainly be available – at least, this would be the perception…

  • Initially it will come as a “assistant” tool (I guess this is how it came to "assist" lectors, coders/developers some time ago).
  • Then tools will evolve and become more powerful.
  • At some pointin time, immediate decision makers will see benefits in introducing  more "reliable" tools instead of us. 
  • The rest of the story – I =guess, we all know…

While it will take time this “future” start happening everywhere (it is close, but not “that” close) – road is clear, right?

In this future, if “I” am replaced with “AI” (or robot, in general), what will stay (for me)? But, things do not end here…

Problem or…

Things are never simple (as I was already trying to say) – replacing the whole class of professionals (whether those are junior developers or coders) or some other professionals will leave a “gap”…

If fact what will become also clear over time – controlling and auditing of AI will emerge as a major issue.

  • Our current AI hallucinate even now when they are fed and trained with knowledge base we humans produced…
  • Next generation of AI will be trained on knowledge current AI is generating, taking all current hallucinations (we miss to correct) as facts, and start adding some new “hallucinations”. This problem will increase exponentially. New generation of people (in a very near future) will take for granted all these things (many take them for granted even now).

Controlling and auditioning of AI and thus correcting AI “decisions” will become increasingly difficult in future. But. some sort of awareness will (most likely) emerge.

… Or opportunity?

Once we become aware of the problem, that usually creates some sort of reaction. And yes, in a good-old EA fashion, we may actually see this rather as an opportunity to improve (ourselves?):

  • As a result of the accumulated hallucinations – I would not be surprised we start gradually returning to “classical education” (detoxicated from AI and even search engines), first as “fashionable” trend, then as a mainstream need.
  • I expect (or hope), exactly all these problems might lead to creating some completely new professions, which will be very (much) demanded in future – people (in various domains) armed with “classical education”, but also capable to understand and work on improving/correcting AI.
  • Some regulations might also pop-up, as we might simply understand that AI (just like other scientific achievements we did) should not be leading toward ultimate “reshaping” (or some would say even “destruction”) of our society (by replacing all of us, or taking resources from use etc.), but toward improving the society and giving us new opportunities. Who knows – maybe…
  • Or more realistically – we cannot replace “all” (or majority?). Things are very simple – if I do not work, I do not earn, thus I do not spend… Who will “fuel” the economy? 

If fact, all this might not be so bad after all? As an outcome, new generations of human professions might start appearing – streaming toward continuous collaboration with AI on “equal” basis, supporting each other, and being aware of the strength and weakness of the other. This would not be a one-way street (as it seems, it is now) but controlled coexistence.

… Although, it will (most certainly) take time until we reach this level of maturity…

Of course, things might be changed again (dramatically?) with the creation of the Self-Aware AI – I guess, an ultimate goal (some) scientists/investors are looking for…

Prologue

There are many versions of the “future”… We’ve heard all the “great” ones, from various marketing channels, and sometimes based on some real good use-cases (let’s be honest). We’ve heard a lot about all those “bad” ones as well, starting from AI taking too many jobs from people, AI is “this or that”, it “hallucinates too much” etc. We’ve heard a lot about overconsumption of water and energy by AI Data Centers. We’ve heard also about issues (becoming now hot topic as well) on “digital sovereignty” – as “he” who controls AI, controls more than just “tools”…

But we might (try) to see some opportunities as well. After all, in all cases future is bringing changes – no use of complaining, changes are fact. The future is not written – we are writing it now. And how good are we in writing good future, this is perhaps the biggest question… So, we better start thinking about what future we want!

If we exclude “great” marketing scenarios, “bad” scenarios (as we just do not want ii?), what do we do to make those “good”, but realistic scenarios, happen? How do we design AI to be really supportive, not destructive and yet we also “earn” something (“the thing” which makes world go ‘round)?

  • Probably we will start by setting some grounding governance rules – yes, governance is the key. And yes, EA for AI maturity has pivotal role in this process. Those rules can determine the future we will have… Not a small button on the shoulder, right? But we need to do it, we need to be part of this – this is not just about “good” or “bad” use-case, this is about “right” or “wrong” application of AI.
  • AI is an asset, water is resource, as well as people – resources must be at the first place. We do not have to introduce AI into every sphere of our life, at least not now. Let's use AI where it "makes sense", let's prioritize – clearly something EA, working on AI use-cases, can/should influence.
  • Democratizing AI by removing governmental controls (easier said than done) – sorry we need sovereign freedom, not another control mechanism, so something has to be done here as well. Maybe we need more competition, more divergency? Okay, here as well EA can have impact, as design choices can/should take into considerations all associated risks – and let me tell you – "decree" driven AI tools, are risk. 

I am only sharing my 2-cents… And remember, a pessimist is an optimist with experience…

---

Disclaimer: When I talk about AI, I express only my personal view. I am neither against nor pro AI – I am very much in favor of scientific and engineering progress, but I also call for a caution and proper regulations.

entry.title: Re: Learning token server deprecation (2027)
entry.content:
@EricAbrams thank you for your feedback.
1. we couldn't communicate earlier because the work to adopt OIDC for Learning wasn't completed. This is in part the reason for pushing out the token deprecation and allow more time for our customers to transition. 
The Help document pages have now been updated to suggest both solutions (but at the time it was accurate to recommend oAuth 2.0 only since Learning wasn't yet enhanced to work with OIDC).

2. there is no deprecation announcement on this topic as of yet. But our Platform colleagues have been recommending OIDC for Platform integrations since 2H2024: Introducing API Authentication with IAS as token p... - SAP Community this is the reason for us to recommend moving to OIDC directly for our customers who haven't yet transitioned and avoid repeating the process when the deprecation comes.

entry.title: Re: Submitting XML via Mail with SAP DRC and SAP ERP
entry.content: Hi Günter, but why do i need SAP DRC Cloud Edition ? I thought i just need DRC Cloud Edition for communcation with the Peppol network. We just want to send out the xml or zugferd file via mail directly to the customer. Thanks. Maurice

entry.title: Unable to return valid error msg from SO enh to BAPI/OData response in S/4HANA.
entry.content:
We are updating Sales Orders via OData,

where the call flow is: CPI → OData Service → DPC Extension Class → BAPI_SALESORDER_CHANGE

Inside this BAPI execution, business validations are implemented in EXIT_SAPMV45A_003 (sales order processing includes).When a validation fails: We raise a hard-coded error message

(MESSAGE e...)The message is available in SY-MSG*

Execution stops immediately BAPI RETURN table is NOT populated

As a result, DPC Extension cannot capture the error CPI receives no meaningful error message

 

This makes it impossible to propagate validation errors back to the external system.

Actual Behavior

Messages raised in EXIT_SAPMV45A_003:Appear only in SY-MSG*

Do not reach BAPI RETURN Cause silent failure from OData perspective 

No standard BAdI / enhancement clearly supports returning validation messages for this scenario.

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 17 - Schema evolution testing
RPR_ABAP_SOURCE_SCAN Does Not Search Text Elements / Text Symbols
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

While analyzing a requirement to identify occurrences of a company name in custom developments, I noticed that the standard report RPR_ABAP_SOURCE_SCAN does not return results when the text is maintained in program Text Elements (Text Symbols).

Example:

- A company name exists in a Text Symbol of a custom program.
- Running RPR_ABAP_SOURCE_SCAN with the company name as the search string returns no results.
- The same text is found when reading the program text pool using READ TEXTPOOL.

My understanding is that RPR_ABAP_SOURCE_SCAN scans ABAP source code but does not search Text Pools (Text Symbols, Selection Texts, List Headers, etc.).

Questions:

1. Is this an expected limitation of RPR_ABAP_SOURCE_SCAN?
2. Is there any SAP standard tool that can search Text Elements across programs?
3. How do you typically search for a text maintained in Text Symbols across custom developments?

As a workaround, I created a utility report using READ TEXTPOOL to scan text symbols in custom programs and it successfully identifies the occurrences.

Looking forward to your insights.

Thanks,
Aravindhan Chandran

entry.title: deploy config issue in BAS
entry.content:
HI Team,

entry.title: Incidencia en sistema TEST a la hora de contabilizar
entry.content:
Hola,

Tenemos una incidencia en el sistema TEST a la hora de contabilizar y es que nos aparece el siguiente error "Local currencies for company code 2830 cannot be completely determined ". 
 
Hemos revisado la configuración de sociedades en el CBC y todas las sociedades financieras tienen asignada su moneda. Además hemos revisado, la configuración de monedas a nivel de ledger y tambien es correcta.

¿Nos podéis ayudar a resolver este error? No podemos contabilizar nada en el sistema TEST.

Also, you could check the blog with Time Valuation - https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blog-posts-by-sap/how-to-setup-time-evaluation-in-time-tracking/ba-p/13545654

entry.title: group reporting standard Equity pickup - how to schedule back ground jobs
entry.content:
Hi All

We are implementing standard equity pick up for one of the client on their S4 V2022 on prem system. SAP has provided standard apps for generating the std EPU and another app to load the GR postings. the Std EPU app places the files in presentation server and the other app picks this file from presentation server and posts into ACDOCU table.

client wants to schedule these as background jobs which runs at particular interval. as these apps places or receives files from presentation server. how to automate this as background job.

please let me know how to acheive it.

Thanks

Hi Thomas,

Thank you very much for your detailed response and the comprehensive information. I believe that if we follow the steps carefully, the issue should be resolved.

However, as the information is quite extensive and includes multiple links, it is a bit challenging to go through everything and validate the setup. I would really appreciate it if you could provide a concise, step-by-step sequence of actions. This would make it much easier for me to follow and verify the current configuration.

Best regards,
Om Prakash Jha

entry.title: Note 3754927 S4CORE
entry.content:
HI team ,

 

Need to know the note 3754927  is applicable for S4CORE version 108 or not ?

entry.title: Re: Transformation Mapping IPS Groups to SAP Datasphere P...
entry.content: If you really want to see an example I can give you one but you will need to put some work on top of it to create your own transformation. Take for example a group with display name = SACPPTP_PTS[PROFILE:t.EOJZ6P:ZDSQ_PTP_Bus_Viewer]. Then we have a transformation that associates the PTP_PTS Team in SAC to the user using sac.group.prefix SACP and "regex": "^%sac.group.prefix%([^\\[]+)\\[[^\\]]+\\]$" and a transformation for Datasphere that uses "regex": "\\[(PROFILE:[^\\]]+)\\]" on the same display name.

entry.title: Re: Introduction to Next-Gen SAP Ariba
entry.content:

Starting this month, we now have a dedicated page for the list of new features released even for the Next-gen SAP Ariba.
As on Jun 12, 2026, we have a list of 34 features released across the solution suite.

entry.title: Re: Business Parnter Replication 986 - BP update does not trigger replication automatically
entry.content: @raskolnico We are facing the same issue. Have you found a solution?

entry.title: SAC Writeback to Datasphere
entry.content:
We are testing the ability for SAP Analytics Cloud to write data to SAP Datasphere. We have encountered an error when trying to do this. 

Step 1 - Created an OAuth client in SAC

Step 2 - Configured Datasphere with connection details, OAuth 2.0 authentication, Grant Type of Client Credentials, provided OAuth Token Endpoint, OAuth Response Type = token, OAuth Token Request Content Type: URL encoded.

Step 3 - Provided Client ID and Secret

Step 4 - Entered Name and Description

Step 5 - Received Connection Created Successfully message. Connection is listed under Datasphere Connections screen.

Step 6 - Click Validate the Connection. ERROR - Connection couldn't be established: Data flows can't be used because of errors in the connection; Replication flows are not supported; Remote tables are disabled. Data Flows: Cause: [CloudDataIntegration] Cannot connect to service because Failed to fetch OAuth2 token. Token endpoint response HTTP/1.1 401 Unauthorized["error": "invalid_client","error_description":"Unauthorized grant type]

entry.title: Introducing Our Latest Course: Positioning SAP Commerce Cloud, Cloud ERP Edition
entry.content:
Are you looking to expand your knowledge of SAP Commerce Cloud and its integration with Cloud ERP? We are excited to announce the launch of our latest course, Positioning SAP Commerce Cloud, Cloud ERP Edition, now available on learning.sap.com!

 

🧩What is SAP Commerce Cloud, Cloud ERP Edition?

SAP Commerce Cloud, Cloud ERP Edition is a powerful and comprehensive e-commerce solution designed to help businesses deliver exceptional customer experiences across all channels. It is a pre-integrated, out-of-the-box solution that combines the capabilities of SAP Commerce Cloud with SAP's Cloud ERP, enabling businesses to streamline their operations, reduce costs, and accelerate their digital transformation journey. With its robust set of features and functionalities, SAP Commerce Cloud, Cloud ERP Edition empowers businesses to create personalized, seamless, and engaging shopping experiences for their customers.

⭐ Why Should You Take This Course?

This course is designed for sales professionals, presales consultants, and anyone looking to gain a deeper understanding of SAP Commerce Cloud, Cloud ERP Edition. By taking this course, you will:

  • Gain a competitive edge - Develop a thorough understanding of SAP Commerce Cloud, Cloud ERP Edition and its unique value proposition, enabling you to better position and sell the solution to potential customers.
  • Enhance your knowledge - Learn about the key features, functionalities, and benefits of SAP Commerce Cloud, Cloud ERP Edition, and how it differs from other SAP Commerce Cloud editions.
  • Improve your sales skills - Discover effective strategies and techniques for positioning and selling SAP Commerce Cloud, Cloud ERP Edition to a variety of customer profiles and industries.
  • Stay ahead of the competition - Keep up to date with the latest developments and innovations in the SAP Commerce Cloud, Cloud ERP Edition landscape, ensuring you are always one step ahead of the competition.

🎯 Learning Objectives

Upon completing this course, you will be able to:

  • Understand the key features and functionalities of SAP Commerce Cloud, Cloud ERP Edition.
  • Identify the target customer profiles and industries for SAP Commerce Cloud, Cloud ERP Edition.
  • Articulate the unique value proposition of SAP Commerce Cloud, Cloud ERP Edition to potential customers.
  • Apply effective sales strategies and techniques to successfully sell SAP Commerce Cloud, Cloud ERP Edition.

📘Course Structure

This course is structured into two units, each covering a specific aspect of SAP Commerce Cloud, Cloud ERP Edition. Here is a brief overview of what you will learn in each unit:

  • Unit 1: Introducing SAP Commerce Cloud, Cloud ERP Edition: In this unit, you will explore the challenges faced in current commerce solutions and discover how SAP Commerce Cloud, Cloud ERP Edition addresses these challenges.

  • Unit 2: Connecting SAP Commerce Cloud, Cloud ERP Edition to End-to-End Business Processes: In this unit, you will discover how SAP Commerce Cloud, Cloud ERP Edition connects to end-to-end business processes.

🏅Earn a Knowledge Badge

Upon successful completion of the Positioning SAP Commerce Cloud, Cloud ERP Edition course, you will earn a badge that you can showcase on your professional profile, such as LinkedIn. This badge is a testament to your knowledge and expertise in SAP Commerce Cloud, Cloud ERP Edition and will help you stand out in the job market and demonstrate your commitment to professional development.

🚀Get Started Today!

Ready to take your knowledge of SAP Commerce Cloud, Cloud ERP Edition to the next level? Enroll in our latest course, Positioning SAP Commerce Cloud, Cloud ERP Edition, today and start your learning journey!

You can find the course here: Positioning SAP Commerce Cloud, Cloud ERP Edition

📢Spread the Word!

Help us share this exciting update with partners, colleagues, and other stakeholders.

📝We Value Your Feedback

After completing the certification, please take a moment to share your feedback. Your insights help us continuously improve the learning experience.
 
Two easy ways to share feedback!
  1. Click “Share Feedback” to submit your thoughts. 

     

     

     

     

  2. In the “Notification Center” under “Learning” you can select the congratulatory email after completing course or learning journey and click “Feedback” to share your thoughts.

entry.title: Re: The Future of ABAP is Here: VS Code ADT, Zero-Config MCP, and AI Co-pilots
entry.content:
if you are still getting the "No System Configurations Found" error with 1.0.1 you can look at the output window for adt (drop down) and see what the error is.  Cert issues can cause some drama in some cases.  It is easy to edit the .json file and create the configurations directly such as:

"destinations": [
{
"id": "xxxx",
"protocol": "rfc",
"properties": {
"systemId": "xxx",
"ssoEnabled": "false",
"messageServer": "xxx.com",
"client": "100",
"language": "EN",
"messageServerPort": "xxxx",
"user": "xxxx",
"sncType": "0",
"group": "PUBLIC"
}
},...

entry.title: Re: Trial account -Unable to login into SAP BTP Cloud Ide...
entry.content:
All are getting the same issue in CIS. They are working on it. It take some time. 
Thank you

entry.title: Re: Purchase order workflow trigger on Condition types
entry.content:
Good day @Srikanth230596 

Thank you for your question.

The standard Manage Workflows for Purchase Orders app does not provide purchase order pricing condition types such as Freight, Basic Customs Duty, SWS Charges, or Anti Dumping Duty as delivered workflow start conditions.

The app supports approval workflow based on the start and step conditions delivered for purchase orders, such as company code, purchasing organisation, purchasing group, document type, account assignment criteria, and value related conditions.

Therefore, you cannot directly selection a pricing condition type such as Freight or Customs Duty as a standard precondition.

The recommended approach is for you to use a custom workflow prediction that evaluates the purchase order pricing conditions. You can define custom start and step conditions for flexible workflows through an applicable Business Add In implementation in the Custom Logic app.

See the following reference for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/46c9e5b6ba7a4feeb920ca1cbad68c73.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/31cc91282192493cbb651c132d966811.html?locale=en-US

The custom logic should do the following:

  1. Read the purchase order pricing elements.
  2. Check whether at least one relevant condition type (e.g., Freight value, Basic Customs Duty, etc) is present.
  3. Return a selectable workflow condition such as PO Contains Relevant Charges = Yes.
  4. Use this custom condition in Manage Workflows for Purchase Orders as the workflow start condition.
  5. Define the required approver and approval steps.

You may also define separate conditions when different approval paths are required, for example:

  • Freight condition exists,
  • Custom duty conditions exist,
  • Anti dumping duty condition exists,
  • Total additional charges exceed a specified amount or percentage          

Before you go ahead with development, confirm the actual technical condition type keys used in your pricing procedure. The descriptions alone are not sufficient because the custom logic must evaluate the technical condition type and, where required, its value or percentage.

You can carry out the following test:

Create a test purchase order that contains one of the relevant condition types and use the workflow simulation function in Manage Workflows for purchase orders.  

What should happen is the custom workflow should be selected when the configured pricing condition is present and the fallback workflow is selected when none of those conditions exists.

See the following reference for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/46c9e5b6ba7a4feeb920ca1cbad68c73.html?locale=en-US

             

Let me know if you have further question.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Trial Cloud Identity Services: first administrator never received activation email
entry.content: Thanks for sharing the detailed troubleshooting steps. Since the subscription was recreated and the onboarding email still wasn’t triggered, this looks more like a provisioning or initial admin setup issue rather than an email delivery problem. It would help if the IAS team could verify whether the administrator account was actually created in the backend and, if needed, re-send the activation email for the tenant. Hope this gets resolved soon.

entry.title: Re: Supplier evaluation by inspection lot
entry.content:
Dear @yasmine_bennani,

In Public Cloud Inspection Lot is a standard supplier evaluation criterion. It is not manually scored in the Supplier Evaluation Weighting and Scoring app this app is used to configure the weighting/scoring logic for criteria such as inspection lot, quantity, time, price, questionnaire and quality notifications

More details below:

SAP Help Supplier Evaluation Weighting and Scoring

The inspection-lot score itself is calculated from QM inspection results. SAP confirms that Supplier Evaluation by Quality Inspection Lot determines the supplier quality score based on QM inspection results. Each inspection lot is recorded, and the purchasing document and item are used to track the supplier. On the QM side, the usage decision / selected set settings can carry a quality score, so the supplier-evaluation quality result is driven by completed inspection lots and their QM quality scores, not by manual scoring in the supplier-evaluation app. The score is calculated from the previous year to date

More details below:

SAP Help Supplier Evaluation by Quality (Inspection Lot)

SAP Help Creating and Editing Selected Sets

Therefore the greyed-out Inspection Lot criterion is not where the score is maintained manually. In your screen the 20% weighting factor determines how much the inspection-lot quality score contributes to the supplier's overall weighted evaluation score, alongside the other active criteria (quantity, time, price, quality notification, questionnaire)

The Inspection Lot criterion is system-managed, and the score is derived from QM inspection lot results. This is why it is not maintained manually like variance-based scoring for Quantity, Time or Price. What you can still adjust is the Weighting Factor and whether the criterion is active that is the lever to control how strongly inspection-lot quality affects the result

More details below:

SAP Help Operational Supplier Evaluation

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: R3C_ARCHIVING OBJECT: FI_DOCUMNT - No store Job in SARA
entry.content:
No store job is found for archiving object FI_DOCUMNT in R3C, despite the successful completion of both the write (WRI) and delete (SUB) jobs. The data has been confirmed as ready for archiving.

As SARA is a standard SAP transaction, this issue cannot be addressed through ABAP analysis alone. Further clarification is required to determine why the store job is not being generated or displayed when attempting to write/archive data in SARA.

entry.title: Re: Plant Abroad Functionality for Non-EU and Non-GCC Countries in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content: @Kishan_Pujara was your ABAP team able to find the enhancement point for determining the tax procedure based on reporting country and company code country for non-EU scenarios? Could you please share details? Thank you!

entry.title: Re: EWM Pick by WO influence source and destination
entry.content:
I would first challenge the process requirement here: why is it necessary to keep several warehouse tasks within one warehouse order if those tasks are not allowed to be moved together?

 

From my understanding, a warehouse order with multiple WTs represents a bundled work package for the resource. If the resource can carry multiple items, the standard RF behavior makes sense: first confirm the source steps, then move the goods together and confirm the destinations.

 

If the process requires strict source-to-destination confirmation per WT, this sounds more like a one-WT-per-WO scenario. In that case, I would rather control it through the warehouse order creation logic instead of changing the RF confirmation sequence.

entry.title: [SAP Certification] [System Based Assessment] - System exam downtime
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm actually writing this post while waiting for the exam system loading 🤣 

I booked a slot for C_DBADM_2601, from June 21st to June 22nd, I failed to take the exam due to System temporarily unavailable, I rescheduled it for 4 times, and the issue still remained.

Then today is the fifth time, I was lucky to access the exam, but it took me almost one hour to get into BTP cockpit, and then when I tried to do assessment, all the process took too long, even hit a button took like 30m to response.

Sure thing it doesn't relate to my network, the exam was taken in region eu10, and my work on SAP BAS is in eu10 too, they are in downtime these days; while other region (like us10 - I tested with trial account) doesn't

I created a case 2 days ago, I kept updating issues until now, but seems no one cares to reply about this issue

Case ID: 5000371611

entry.title: Re: New POD 2.0 Framework: Certificate Check
entry.content:
Hi @ThanosG, DM mostly uses explicitly named users, for traceability purposes.

The Certification Check API described above and a few others, support UserID input.
So, you can easily use new POD 2.0 capabilities, like Data Scanning (I have another blog on that) or add a Dialog to the Start button flow, to collect the UserID and submit to the API, to validate specific users.

Shared Work Stations is a common requirement indeed, and there are some ongoing discussions on how to improve support of that, but keep in mind user traceability must not be overlooked.

Hope it helps! Br, Manoel

entry.title: Is there any program to synch the ACDOCP cost element category 42 values to CO tables in S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Hello SAP team,

We tried to transfer the values from ACDOCP to CO tables by using the program R_FINS_PLAN_TRANS_CO_S4H_2_ERP and we got the  error message.

entry.title: Re: 创建了项目参数文件：有收入的项目后，对项目进行报工，并进行了作业的实际成本率的计算后，在运行实际成本核算后，无法将作业的实际成本计入到项目中，希望得到您的帮助和支持？
entry.content:
Hello @ lemon-xue

Thank you for your question.

The phrase “project parameter file” is unclear to me. Could you clarify whether this concerns a customer project or an investment/internal project, and please specify:

  1. The exact Fiori app or process you used to calculate the actual activity rate.
  2. The app used for actual cost allocation or actual costing
  3. Whether the activity confirmation exists on the project and shows the correct quantity
  4. The exact error message or application log result
  5. Whether you expect the cost on the project, the profitability segment, or the revenue recognition result.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Open QP Settlements data migration – Responsibility,...
entry.content:
Good day @valdir_junior453f80,
Good day @Chris1973 

you can also use the Explore Migration Objects app to check on available objects within your system based on the activated scope.

Check my blogs on the EMO app. It is better to check for existing objects via this app than the migration cockpit. The enhanced app within 2602.4 also includes new functionalities that displays you if an object is available: What’s New in the Explore Migration Objects App: R... - SAP Community

As it is released with a CFD you may need to activate the enhanced first.

The total overview on available migration objects can be easily found on the respective Help Portal page: https://help.sap.com/s4_CE_MO.

The object you are searching for is not an existing object and is also not in an pipeline to be build for future releases.

BR

Frank

entry.title: Re: Hide Award Configuration Fields from Workflow Approva...
entry.content:
Hi @deepaksajjan unfortunately colleagues and product confirmed today that your requirement is not possible in the product at this time and would require an enhancement request.

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Adding Custom Field in LTMC Template
entry.content:
Hi @mynthan_karuppasamy20ee52 

as you tagged Public Cloud, I guess you want to search for a Public Cloud solution. The mention online course is only for OP and Private Cloud. For Public Cloud, neither the migration object nor its BAPI is being released for custom field usage within the migration object. BAPIs are not compatible to use key-user-extensibility with the migration cockpit as they are using their on extension model. Nevertheless the used BAPI is also not assigned to a Business Context.

Using retired term "LTMC" within a public cloud tagged question often confuses people as LTMC is retired for over six years now in Public Cloud and since 2021 release in OP. Since that time the former transaction and web-dynpro app LTMC cannot be used anymore. Instead the migration cockpit based on the Fiori app musted be used. If you ask a question using term LTMC you will mostly get answers for OP and Private Cloud. the new app has nothing in common with the old transaction LTMC.

What is possible within the limited Model my migration object within Public Cloud is described within the Help Portal: Migration Object Modeler | SAP Help Portal and the linked training material: Migrate Data Using Staging Tables | SAP Help Portal

 

BR

Frank

entry.title: Re: Step activity API does not provide "TIme processe...
entry.content:
Hi,

I just tried /sfc/v1/stepActivity after processing a given SFC 4 times, for a given operation, and it returns 'timesProcessed' as '4', so it works just fine, see below.

From /sfc/v1/stepActivity, you can check timesProcessed definition as: The number of times the SFC was processed at the step due to non-conformance.

So, my test case was:

  • Start an SFC on a given Operation
  • Raise a Nonconformance with a Disposition Routing assigned, which dispositions the SFC to Rework
  • Start and Complete Rework operation, which returns the SFC to the original operation
  • Repeat 4 times

Then, I called /sfc/v1/stepActivity which returned timesProcessed as 4, just fine.

Hope it helps! Br, Manoel

entry.title: Re: Introduction to Tendering(Sub-Contracting) in SAP Transportation Management
entry.content:
This is an amazing blog! I am new to the TMS process and at the end of this blog I understood the very basics of a Tendering and the Carrier RFQ process. Kudos Rajashiva! 
Very well put and explained in simple terms. 
Do you also by any chance have the SAP Backend table details for each these steps mentioned? That would be super helpful as I am trying to get hold of the actual technical table on where i could get the subcontracting details, like each RFQ sent to different carriers for a single FO and charges for that RFQ quoted. 
Any leads would be appreciated a lot! TIA. 

Cheers! 

#Tendering #sub-contracting SAP Transportation Management

entry.title: Re: Bug in ABAP Language
entry.content: Did you check the SAP Notes? Such an old bug would probably have a fix already. If not, open an incident with a S-user.

entry.title: Re: Periodic billing for a service material
entry.content:
Dear @AnaMaria1,

In Public Cloud the billing plan type cannot be changed manually from 90 to 92 in a sales order item for a service material. SAP states that the billing plan type is determined from the sales document/item configuration and cannot be changed in the sales document. The Public Cloud sales-order billing-plan setup is controlled via configuration such as Assign Billing Plan Types to Sales Document Types and SAP documents billing plan type 90 as the standard/default setup for sales orders

More details below:

SAP Help – Billing Plans

SAP Help – Configuring Billing Plans

SAP Help – App Implementation: Manage Sales Orders – Version 2

SAP Help – Billing Plans for Advanced Down Payment Processing in Sales Orders

Periodic billing is supported in SAP but in Public Cloud the documented standard service scenario for periodic billing is the service contract, where billing is triggered according to the billing plan in each service contract item. Since service contracts are not to be used in this requirement, billing rule 6 periodic billing rent cannot be enabled in a normal sales order by switching billing plan type 90 to 92

More details below:

SAP Help – Service Contract Items

SAP Help – Billing of Service Transactions

Summary:

This is standard Public Cloud behavior. The billing plan type is configuration-driven and cannot be manually changed from 90 to 92 in the sales order

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 17 - Schema evolution testing
Re: SAP TechEd 2026: Registration is live!
entry.content:
I'm glad the Community Club House is there.

My favorite quote about SAP TechEd - "it's not about marketing, but what the product can do".

Enjoy

entry.title: Re: Activate & Monitor: Your S/4HANA Private Cloud with SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
@NandhiniRavisankar 

S/4HANA Private Cloud + Cloud ALM monitoring:

  • In S/4: run /n/SDF/ALM_SETUP, activate push data provider for your use cases, ensure background job is scheduled.

  • In Cloud ALM: open Integration & Exception Monitoring, select your S/4 system, configure alerts (e.g., short dumps, broken IDocs) and route to email/Teams/ServiceNow.

Key notes:

  • Included in RISE with SAP for Private Cloud (no extra license).

  • Uses PUSH from S/4; no Cloud Connector needed.

  • If you clone the system, preserve monitoring setup tables so metrics stay correct.

entry.title: The Resources You Need for SAP Customer Loyalty Management — All in One Place
entry.content:
Picture this: your organisation has decided to implement SAP Customer Loyalty Management. You understand the solution can handle loyalty programmes, brand partnerships, member wallets, and AI-powered experiences. But where does the work actually start? And how do you make sure nothing critical is missed between evaluation and go-live?
 
It's a familiar situation for many project teams. That's why we've put together a curated resource guide — so you have what you need, at every stage of your project.
 

A phase-by-phase guide, built for your journey

 

The "Foundational Resources for SAP Customer Loyalty Management" guide is now available inside the SAP Customer Experience Value Map, part of SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps. Our product and topic experts have selected and organised the resources you need, from your first evaluation through to live operations.

You can access the guide directly here: Foundational resources for SAP Customer Loyalty Management*
 
Spoiler
*Note: Some resources within the guide are hosted on SAP Learning and are marked with an asterisk (*). Accessing them is free, but you'll need to complete a one-time registration with your S-user.
 

What's inside

The guide follows six phases that reflect how a real Customer Loyalty Management project unfolds — from building the business case to day-to-day operations.
 
Discover — Understand the solution and its business value. If you're evaluating the solution or preparing a business case, this is your starting point. You'll find a product overview video, the official SAP Help page, an introduction to Loyalty and Brand Programs, and the SAP Road Map Explorer for Customer Loyalty Management. There are also AI enablement sessions on Joule and SAP Business AI, relevant across the SAP CX portfolio.
 
Prepare — Plan your project and set up for success. A well-structured project starts before the first configuration task. This section covers cloud adoption principles, SAP CX security baselines, SAP Cloud ALM for project governance, and the fundamentals of organisational change management. So your team has what it needs before implementation begins.
 
Explore — Validate the solution and define your configuration. When you're in fit-to-standard workshops, detailed documentation matters. This section points you to the SAP Customer Loyalty Management Help Portal, along with focused guides on Club Management, Communication Topics, and Configuring Points Earning Limits.
 
Realize — Build, integrate, and test your solution. This is where the configuration work happens. You'll find step-by-step documentation for initial system setup, customer identity configuration, adding promotions, and defining rewards. It also covers how to use SAP Cloud ALM during the build phase and how to explore Joule and Business AI capabilities within SAP CX.
 
Deploy — Prepare for go-live and stay ready for each release. Go-live preparation takes more than technical readiness. This section helps your team stay aligned on release notes, roadmap updates, and the SAP CX Release Navigator, alongside change management resources to support user adoption before and after the cutover.
 
Run — Operate, optimise, and maximise adoption. Once you're live, the focus shifts to operations and continuous improvement. You'll find links to SAP CX Live Webcasts & Replays, Cloud Availability Notifications, the Customer Influence Portal for submitting product ideas, the Customer Insights Dashboard, and a quick-reference info sheet for Joule in SAP for Me.
 
 

Ready to explore more? 

 
Visit us on SAP Community for more information and to get access. Don't forget you can "Request a Call" with one of our topic experts to connect you with the right enablement resources, fast.

entry.title: SAP DRC France on SAP ERP ECC6 EHP6 – Inconsistent eDocument creation in EDOC_COCKPIT
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

We are currently implementing SAP DRC France on SAP ERP ECC6 EHP6 for the upcoming France e-invoicing / e-reporting mandate.

All recommended SAP Notes have been implemented, and the customizing described in SAP Help Portal has been followed for:

  • Electronic Invoicing and Reporting via Approved Platform
  • Electronic Invoicing via Peppol

After running several tests, we are still facing inconsistent behavior in EDOC_COCKPIT.

Current behavior observed:

  • For FI customer invoices, eDocuments are created for French customers with or without SIREN, but not for foreign customers.
  • For SD billing documents, eDocuments are created for French customers without SIREN and for foreign customers, but not for French customers with SIREN.
  • SD credit memos are displayed in the cockpit, but with an incorrect document/process description.
  • FI B2B International invoices and credit memos are not displayed in the cockpit.
  • For one FI B2B International invoice, the document is displayed only when the currency is different from EUR, but with an incorrect description.
  • Full and partial incoming payments posted with F-28 do not generate the expected payment reporting in the cockpit.

Have you encountered similar behavior on SAP ERP / ECC for SAP DRC France?

Do you know if:

  • only the Electronic Invoicing and Reporting via Approved Platform customizing should be maintained for the France B2B mandate;
  • maintaining both customizing nodes can impact eDocument/process determination;
  • there are additional SAP Notes, BC Sets, BAdIs or configuration points to check for FI vs SD determination;
  • specific prerequisites are required for payment reporting from F-28?

Any feedback or return of experience would be appreciated.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Creating and Activating an RFC Connection for SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Editi
entry.content:
@RolandKoxherri 

You’re basically just telling your ERP/S/4 system where the Document and Reporting Compliance service lives, and then flipping a switch in the service to use that connection.

In practice:

  • In BTP, create the service key for Document and Reporting Compliance and copy the drccApiUrl.

  • In your ERP/S/4 (SM59), create an HTTP RFC (type G), set the host to that URL (without https://), port 443, and the right path prefix.

  • Turn on SSL and pick your auth method (OAuth or client cert), save, and test the connection.

  • Then go into the Document and Reporting Compliance UI, enter that RFC name, and activate it.

Once that’s done, the service can call back to your system to pull data for statutory reports and submissions.

entry.title: Re: Assignment date and completion date in the same report
entry.content: In addition to the user learning plan audit report, you may need to run an additional "learning history" report for events recorded but never assigned to the user.

entry.title: Re: Integrating SAP Cloud Identity Services (Identity Authentication - IAS) with SAP Audit Log Viewe
entry.content:
@Yogananda Can you provide an example of this and which event type(s) show this? We are talking about changes to a specific User P##### I changed their name, their password, etc. Not configuration changes that were already available in the built-in IAS Admin changes log. 

What @Timsen and I are both experiencing seems to be the same thing - we are not seeing these user change audit events showing in the log viewer. Trying to figure out why is providing difficult because of the lack of options/roles/configurability in this tool. It is supposed to be simple but then there are no results. I'm opening an SAP ticket to see what is going on in  my system but an example of what to filter on would also be nice to ensure we are looking for the right things in the garbled audit log events that are difficult enough to read what it is trying to say by Humans 🙂

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Paraguay - Asunción 2026
entry.content:
Nos complace anunciar la segunda edición del SAP Inside Track en Paraguay, que este año se realizará en Asunción.


La primera edición, celebrada en Ciudad del Este en noviembre de 2025, marcó un punto de partida importante para nuestra comunidad. Reunió a profesionales de distintos puntos del país y de la región, y confirmó algo que ya intuíamos: existe en Paraguay una comunidad SAP activa, con ganas de compartir conocimiento y de construir vínculos que trascienden lo profesional. Ese primer encuentro nos dejó aprendizajes, nuevos contactos y, sobre todo, la convicción de que valía la pena dar el siguiente paso.
Ese paso nos trae hoy a la capital.

El SAP Inside Track es una iniciativa 100% comunitaria, organizada por y para los profesionales de SAP. No es un evento comercial ni corporativo: es un espacio de intercambio genuino, donde quienes trabajamos día a día con la tecnología nos reunimos para compartir experiencias reales, discutir desafíos concretos y aprender unos de otros.

Llevar el SIT a Asunción responde a un objetivo claro: seguir ampliando y conectando a la comunidad SAP paraguaya. Queremos que más personas tengan la oportunidad de participar, de exponer su trabajo y de encontrarse con colegas que comparten los mismos intereses. El evento contará nuevamente con presentaciones de expertos locales e internacionales, junto con espacios pensados para el networking y la conversación.


La participación de toda la comunidad es lo que da sentido a este evento. Ya sea como speaker, como asistente o como colaborador, hay un lugar para cada persona interesada en formar parte.

  • Fecha: 7 de noviembre de 2026
  • Lugar: A confirmar
  • Inscripciones: A confirmar
  • Agenda: A confirmar

 

Social Media:

INSTAGRAM SITPY

LINKEDIN SITPY

 

Etiquetas:
#Paraguay #SITPY #SITPY2026

entry.title: Re: Request for RLM document ECC to S/4 HANA.
entry.content:
Hello @thotasai_tejaswi2b1d5c  - Pls refer the Simplification List, you can find all the relevant information over there.

 

thanks,

Ankur

entry.title: Re: No Search help for 'Incoterm Location 1' field INCO2_...
entry.content:
Hello @aritra_sinhad288df  - As this is a Free Text Field, that means you can maintain the values as per your need. 

 

Thanks,

Ankur

entry.title: Re: Posting period Issue
entry.content:
Hello @KSR_2024  - There is no way you can do that, you need to get in touch with Finance team to post the entries directly on the GLs, but if you really wanted to do this you need to initialise the posting period, which is a very risky and not recommended way but the steps are - 

  1. Goto Transaction code "OX18"
  2. remove the company code plant Assignment
  3. Goto "OMSY" initialise the period as per your requirement.
  4. Post the GR
  5. Close the period and change it to current period.

Thanks,

Ankur
